title: Dreams, Emptiness, Deception
content:
{{Act Infobox |image = Dreams, Emptiness, Deception Travel Log Preview Aether.png|Aether Dreams, Emptiness, Deception Travel Log Preview Lumine.png|Lumine |type = Archon |chapter = Chapter III |actNum = III |region = Sumeru |prev = The Morn a Thousand Roses Brings |next = King Deshret and the Three Magi |ARReq = 35 |rep = Sumeru |repAmt = 100 |repOrder = 498 }} {{Description|"The world as it is, and all that is in it — it is but a dream, empty and full of lies."
The [[Sabzeruz Festival]] samsara finally comes to an end.
The path to saving the [[Lesser Lord Kusanali|god]] lies within the desert stars.
"I am the [[King Deshret|lord of the scarlet soil]]. My deeds are world-renowned, and even the strong kneel before me!"}} '''Dreams, Emptiness, Deception''' is the third act in [[Chapter III]] of the [[Archon Quests]]. ==Quests== # [[Like a Triumphant Hero]] # [[The Gaze From a Certain God]] # [[The Four at Loggerheads]] ==Summary== ;Like a Triumphant Hero Some time after the end of the Sabzeruz Festival samsara, [[Nahida]] takes over [[Katheryne]]'s body again to speak to the [[Traveler]] and [[Paimon]] when they visit the [[Adventurers' Guild]]. Nahida wants to resolve the matter with the [[Sumeru Akademiya]]'s sages before focusing her attention on [[Irminsul]] and [[The Withering]]. Because the sages have taken careful measures to avoid information leaks from the [[Akasha]], her usual source of information, Nahida proposes that they convince [[Setaria]] — Grand Sage [[Azar]]'s secretary — to reveal their plans. Setaria's genius gave her an exceptional opportunity for a desert-dweller, but this has left her plagued by guilt over the sages' questionable work and her inability to use her studies to help the desert people. If they can make Setaria confront her guilt about her homeland, they will have a mole on the inside. In the guise of Katheryne, Nahida talks to some of Setaria's acquaintances so that the Traveler can imitate them during their conversations with Setaria the next day. The following day, as Setaria goes shopping, the targets' consciousnesses are overridden by the Traveler's, who pretends that each of them has become [[King Deshret]]'s follower. Over the course of the confrontations, Setaria reveals that King Deshret's resurrection was an Akademiya misinformation campaign. A bewildered Setaria flags down [[Corps of Thirty]] member [[Rima]] to warn her that something strange is afoot, but Rima's consciousness is taken over by the Traveler, who continues confronting Setaria. Finally, Setaria cracks and agrees to covertly work against the sages, but even she knows little about the project's full scope. She decides to steal confidential documents and pass them to the Traveler at the Akademiya's entrance the next night. As they approach the meeting point, the Traveler and Nahida's suspicions about the dearth of people prove well-founded when they find a mob of people at the Akademiya entrance, all cheering and whooping. [[Il Dottore]] (The Doctor), No. 2 of the [[Eleven Fatui Harbingers]], reveals himself as the mastermind behind this scene. Setaria's conversation with the Traveler was overheard through a listening device and she was imprisoned, while Dottore conducted an experiment on people's [[Akasha Terminal]]s, planting into their minds the idea that the Traveler has just saved the world and inducing the crowd to start mobbing them. Nahida, in Katheryne's body, steps in front of the Traveler and tells them to run while she handles the situation. In doing so, however, she reveals the scope of her abilities to Dottore. Now that they have learned about the Akademiya's collaboration with the [[Fatui]], the Traveler and Paimon decide to seek out [[Tighnari]] to learn more about the sage's project. ---- ;The Gaze from a Certain God On the way to [[Gandharva Ville]], the Traveler and Paimon repel an ambush by elite Eremite groups hired by Dottore. They find [[Collei]] in the village, whose [[Eleazar]] has worsened and prohibited her from going on expeditions. Tighnari himself uncharacteristically went to [[Pardis Dhyai]] on short notice, so the two head there next. There, they find "Katheryne" — Nahida managed to escape Dottore's grasp at the last second, but she suspects that he will soon find a way to trap her consciousness within the Akasha. While searching for Tighnari, they encounter [[Haypasia]], who believes that she has reached [[Paripurna Life]] and offers to show them what she saw. Instead of [[Greater Lord Rukkhadevata]]'s consciousness, however, they witness Fatui Harbinger [[Scaramouche]]'s memories. Before they can dwell on this further, more Eremites attack them. In the ensuing melee, "Katheryne" is run through by a spear. She touches fingers with the Traveler, and the two's consciousnesses trade bodies. From within Nahida's true body at the [[Sanctuary of Surasthana]], the Traveler witnesses Scaramouche in the process of becoming a god — and Scaramouche witnesses them in turn. By the time they return to their own body, the Traveler finds themself fleeing Pardis Dhyai with Tighnari, who had come to their rescue. While indwelling the Traveler, Nahida left them one final clue before her consciousness was trapped by Dottore: "Even in a moonless night, a shower of starlight can still drown out illusions and lies." After determining that this meant seeking help from the people of the [[Great Red Sand]], the Traveler and Paimon part ways with Tighnari, who decides to stay behind in Pardis Dhyai and continue monitoring Haypasia's condition. ---- ;The Four at Loggerheads At [[Caravan Ribat]], the Traveler and Paimon narrowly escape the Eremites trailing them thanks to Alhaitham's help. Alhaitham tells them that he's still studying the capsule and believes the Traveler might be hiding something from him before offering to go to Aaru Village with them, which they accept. When they arrive at the village, the Traveler senses [[Cyno|someone]] waiting to attack and parries the first strike before they clash with Alhaitham to a standstill. Alhaitham recognizes the man as Cyno, the General Mahamatra. [[Cyno]] demands he reveal the Divine Knowledge Capsule before Alhaitham questions him being alone in the desert, as the other matra were wondering where he went. Suddenly, [[Dehya]] arrives and tries to mediate, although the two men are still insistent on fighting. A sandstorm then kicks up, forcing the group into the village chief's hut when [[Candace|another woman]] calls out to them. Inside, the woman introduces herself as Candace and warns them not to cause a fight in the village again before asking them to explain what happened. Cyno goes first, stating that he put himself in exile after noticing missing data that Azar submitted himself. When he tried to investigate, Azar denied his request, stating that he had no right to judge them. Cyno then reveals that Alhaitham had been tasked with tracking the Traveler, who doesn't deny the accusation, but reveals that he did not accept it, as the sages had promised him divine knowledge, which he found extremely suspicious. Dehya then explains that she was just bored and wanted to return to the village, just as they hear growling outside. Candace leaves the house to handle the monsters. After some time passes, the Traveler and Dehya worry about her condition, so they head out and find her fighting some [[riftwolf|riftwolves]] and join in, repelling the enemy attack. The village chief, [[Uncle Anpu]], then arrives, explaining that the Village Keepers have gone missing recently with no trace. Cyno explains that they're actually the exiled scholars of the Akademiya, as they believe that their crazed ramblings would give a negative psyche to others. Cyno then asks the Traveler to meet him elsewhere for a conversation. At the arranged spot, Cyno explains the history of the Village Keepers; they were initially shunned by the village due to not being useful, but when an earthquake struck one day a mad scholar crouched to the ground with a green light radiating from him, protecting the entire village from any damage. The Traveler believes this to be the Dendro Archon's work, which Cyno believes and is surprised to learn that the Archon walks among the people. Just then, a boy named [[Isak]] appears and asks them to help find his grandpa. Returning to the chief's house, Cyno confirms with Candace about Isak, who explains his past to them. Isak is delighted to learn that they'll be searching for his grandpa, so they begin to look for leads. ==Characters== {{Character Appearances|Dreams, Emptiness, Deception|type=Act}} ==Achievements== {{Achievement Topic Table|quest=Dreams, Emptiness, Deception}} ==Soundtracks== {{Soundtracks by Category Table|type=quest}} ==Gallery== Story Teaser- The "Divine" Will - Genshin Impact Dreams, Emptiness, Deception Event Menu.png|Event Menu Story Card Chapter III Act III.png|Travel Log Card ==Other Languages== {{Other Languages |en = Dreams, Emptiness, Deception |zhs = 迷梦与空幻与欺骗 |zhs_rm = Mímèng yǔ Kōnghuàn yǔ Qīpiàn |zht = 迷夢與空幻與欺騙 |zht_rm = Mímèng yǔ Kōnghuàn yǔ Qīpiàn |zh_tl = Delusion, Illusion, and Deception |ja = 迷夢と虚幻と欺瞞 |ja_rm = Meimu to Kyogen to Giman{{Assumed}} |ja_tl = Delusion, Illusion, and Deception |ko = 미몽과 허상 그리고 기만 |ko_rm = Mimong-gwa Heosang Geurigo Giman |ko_tl = Delusion, Illusion, and Deception |es = Quimeras, ilusiones y engaños |es_tl = Chimeras, Illusions and Deceptions |fr = Rêves, futilité et tromperie |fr_tl = Dreams, Futility, and Deception |ru = Сны, пустота и обман |ru_tl = Dreams, Emptiness, and Deception |th = นิมิต มายา และการลวงหลอก |th_rm = Nimit Maya lae Kan Luang-Lok |th_tl = Premonition, Illusion, and Deception |vi = Ảo Mộng, Trống Rỗng Và Dối Gian |vi_tl = Illusion, Emptiness and Deception |de = Luftschlösser, Illusionen und Betrug |de_tl = {{Wt|castle in the air|Castles in the Air}}, Illusions and Deception |id = Mimpi, Kekosongan, dan Muslihat |id_tl = Dreams, Emptiness, and Deception |pt = Sonhos, Vazio e Engano |pt_tl = Dreams, Emptiness and Deception |tr = Rüyalar, Boşluk, Yanılgı |tr_tl = Dreams, Emptiness, Delusion |it = Sogni, senso di vuoto e inganno |it_tl = Dreams, a Sense of Emptiness and Deception }} ==Change History== {{Change History|3.1}} ==Navigation== {{Archon Quest Navbox|Chapter III}} [[es:Quimeras, ilusiones y engaños]] [[ja:迷夢と虚幻と欺瞞]] [[pl:Sny, pustka, podstęp]] [[pt-br:Sonhos, Vazio e Engano]] [[ru:Сны, пустота и обман]] [[vi:Ảo Mộng, Trống Rỗng Và Dối Gian]]
